title: Quick check: Broad vs narrow targeting

questions.prompt: Why does broad targeting often beat narrow in 2026?

choices.text: Meta's optimization layer (EAR + pixel + CAPI signal) is now precise enough to find the right slice inside a big pool. Narrow interest filters cut the pool the ML has to learn from, fragment delivery, and bet your interest guess beats the algorithm's behavioral inference. Sweet spot is 2-10M; below 1M delivery gets erratic. Broad works only when the creative pre-qualifies — vague creative + broad targeting wastes spend.

questions.explanation: Optimization layer is strong enough that narrow filtering now hurts; creative becomes the filter.

questions.prompt: When does narrow still beat broad?

choices.text: B2B with job title filters (CRM lists or title targeting), regulated verticals (compliance), hyper-local services (zip-code radius), and high-AOV niche where lookalikes-of-buyers convert better than broad. For mass-market DTC, app installs, and lead-gen, broad usually wins by 10-30% CPA after learning phase. Test broad vs narrow head-to-head with identical creative to see which your account is in.

questions.explanation: B2B, regulated, hyper-local, and high-AOV niche still benefit from narrow. Test to be sure.
